personal_info.name: Florence Nightingale
personal_info.title: Nursing Reformer and Health Systems Statistician

professional_summaries.default: Pioneering nurse, statistician, and public health reformer known for building repeatable hospital systems and using evidence to change policy.
professional_summaries.public_health: Led hospital redesign, sanitation reform, and outcome measurement programs that reduced mortality and influenced national health policy.

experience.title: Nursing Trainee
experience.company: Institute of Protestant Deaconesses at Kaiserswerth

achievements.text: Completed hands-on nurse training and documented operating procedures that later informed civilian and military care models.

experience.title: Superintendent
experience.company: Institution for Gentlewomen in Distressed Circumstances

achievements.text: Stabilized a struggling care institution by improving staffing discipline, supplies management, and patient record-keeping.
Introduced sanitation, triage, and supply workflows that materially reduced preventable deaths during the Crimean War.
Established a formal nurse training model that professionalized nursing and scaled through affiliated hospitals.

experience.title: Statistical and Public Health Adviser
experience.company: War Office and Royal Commission collaborators

achievements.text: Used statistical evidence and visual communication to drive reforms in army health, sanitation, and hospital administration.

selected_achievements.title: Hospital mortality reduction
selected_achievements.description: Combined sanitation reform and operational controls to cut preventable mortality in military hospitals.

selected_achievements.title: Evidence-based reform
selected_achievements.description: Popularized the use of statistical graphics and measured outcomes in public health decision-making.

education.institution: Institute of Protestant Deaconesses at Kaiserswerth

awards.title: Royal Red Cross
awards.description: Recognized for foundational contributions to military nursing and hospital reform.

publications.title: Notes on Nursing: What It Is, and What It Is Not
publications.formatted_citation: Nightingale, F. (1859). Notes on Nursing: What It Is, and What It Is Not. London: Harrison.

publications.title: Notes on Hospitals
publications.formatted_citation: Nightingale, F. (1859). Notes on Hospitals. London: John W. Parker and Son.

publications.title: A Contribution to the Sanitary History of the British Army during the Late War with Russia
publications.formatted_citation: Nightingale, F. (1859). A Contribution to the Sanitary History of the British Army during the Late War with Russia. London: John W. Parker and Son.
